children.name: Recurvirostra
children.description: Elegant pied waders with distinctively upturned bills swept through shallow water to catch invertebrates. The Pied Avocet breeds in shallow saline lagoons in southern Sweden — Öland and Gotland — and is expanding northward.

children.name: Recurvirostra avosetta
children.description: An elegant black-and-white wader that sweeps its slender upturned bill sideways through shallow water to filter out invertebrates. It breeds in saline lagoons on Öland, Gotland, and the southern coast and is expanding northward.

children.name: Himantopus himantopus
children.description: A strikingly long-legged black-and-white wader that wades belly-deep on impossibly thin pink legs. A scarce but increasingly frequent southern overshoot to Sweden as its European range shifts north.
